frame_analyses.description: Initial view showing blank frame before task begins
Two graspers visible in frame, right grasper approaching needle on green material
Green Penrose drain with slit visible, needle being manipulated by right instrument

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, controlled approach

frame_analyses.description: Needle being passed through drain at first mark location
Left grasper assisting with suture manipulation, preparing for second pass
Instruments positioned for knot tying, suture tails visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate suture length for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument wrapping suture around left for surgeon's knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper double-wrap technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: First throw being cinched down, maintaining appropriate tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control, knot being properly seated

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed suture with cut ends, task appears complete
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends cut appropriately short

penalties.description: Entry point appears to be approximately 1-2mm from marked location

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw formation with appropriate wrapping

confidence_rationale: Video quality is good but camera angles make precise measurement of suture deviation difficult. Knot formation and technique clearly visible. Task completion and timing well-documented.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation from marks due to camera angle limitations
Precise timing between individual knot throws - estimated from available frames
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with efficient technique and proper knot sequence. All required elements completed correctly with minimal deviation.
strengths: Excellent completion time of 60 seconds - well within expert range
Proper surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle placement at marked locations to minimize deviation penalties
